mnemonics: P.R.E.C.I.S.I.O.N.: Performance Review, Error Calculation, Interpreting Statistical Indicators Of Noise
keyPoints: Precision (repeatability) measures the consistency of repeated measurements under unchanged conditions.
Accuracy (correctness) measures how close a measurement is to the true value, distinct from precision.
High variance in a model's predictions indicates low precision, even if its average accuracy is acceptable.
commonMistakes: Confusing precision (repeatability/consistency) with accuracy (closeness to true value).
Assuming a precise measurement is automatically an accurate one, ignoring potential systematic bias.
